title: digitalis lingulata
intro: Digitalis lingulata, also known as the small foxglove, is a biennial plant in the foxglove family (Scrophulariaceae) and is closely related to the common foxglove (Digitalis purpurea). The plant contains compounds similar to digoxin, a cardiac glycoside with medicinal properties used to treat heart conditions such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. The medicinal properties of Digitalis lingulata have been used to improve heart function, reduce symptoms of heart failure, and lower blood pressure. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or capsules, but caution is advised due to the risk of toxicity and overdose.
